title: Hotfix: make check_complexity import-safe without radon (un-crash pytest collection)
intent: test_complexity_key_collision.py (added in #163) imports app.ci.check_complexity at module top. That module did `try: from radon.complexity import cc_visit; except ImportError: sys.exit(2)` AT IMPORT time. radon is installed only for the debt-gate job, not for the general pytest environments (api-verify, Core CI), so importing it during pytest collection aborted the whole session with INTERNALERROR SystemExit:2 -- masking the entire suite. Root-cause fix: check_complexity imports radon LAZILY (inside check_complexity(), via _load_cc_visit), so importing the module never exits; the CLI main() maps the missing dependency to exit code 2 as before. The test additionally guards its bodies with pytest.importorskip('radon.complexity'). CI tooling only; no product/runtime path touched; CLI exit-code semantics preserved.

diff_articulation.what_changed: check_complexity.py: replace the module-level `try: from radon.complexity import cc_visit; except ImportError: sys.exit(2)` with a lazy `_load_cc_visit()` (raises `_RadonMissing` if radon is absent) called inside check_complexity(); main() catches `_RadonMissing` and returns exit code 2. Importing the module no longer runs radon's import or sys.exit, so it is safe to import during pytest collection in any environment. test_complexity_key_collision.py: add `pytest.importorskip('radon.complexity')` before importing check_complexity (now `# noqa: E402`) to skip the radon-dependent test bodies where radon is absent. CLI behavior (exit 0 OK / 1 violations / 2 runtime error) is unchanged where radon is present.
diff_articulation.why_not_redundant: A module that calls sys.exit() at import time is collection-hostile: ANY importer lacking the dev-only radon dependency crashes the whole pytest session, not just this test. Guarding only the one test (an earlier iteration) would leave the trap armed for the next importer; moving the radon import to call time removes the trap at the source while preserving the CLI's exit-code contract. The importorskip guard remains because the test bodies genuinely exercise the radon-backed complexity walk.

commands_run: import check_complexity with radon forced absent -> imports cleanly, no SystemExit (collection-safe); _load_cc_visit and check_complexity present
services/api/.venv: python -m app.ci.check_complexity --baseline app/ci/complexity_baseline.json --threshold 15 -> OK: No functions exceed complexity 15 (exit 0) -- CLI gate path unchanged
python scripts/ci/check_cbsp21_gate.py --manifest .cbsp21/patch_input.json --changed-files <changed> -> PASS (.cbsp21/ exempt; 2 code files declared; coverage 100% >= 95%)

files.intent: Import radon lazily so importing the module never sys.exit()s; CLI maps missing radon to exit 2
Guard the radon-dependent test bodies with pytest.importorskip('radon.complexity')
